title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Guided Eval Studio
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a safe tool-use decision protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Tool Failure Recovery condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Failure Recovery applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.
